title: Cevi AI — Shared Definitions
description: Canonical shared blocks for Cevi AI fax-extraction schemas. Every category schema inlines equivalent structures so extractors without $ref support (e.g., Gemini structured output) can consume them directly. This file is the single source of truth for downstream code and post-extraction validation. All schemas capture what appears on the fax, not downstream derived fields (patient matching, urgency, review priority, etc.) which are separate pipeline stages.

person_name.description: Person name captured from document. raw_text is the verbatim string exactly as printed; first_name/middle_name/last_name/suffix are parsed components for separate copy-paste into eCW fields. Preserve hyphens, apostrophes, and multi-part surnames (O'Brien, Rodriguez-Hernandez, de la Cruz).

raw_text.description: Verbatim name string exactly as it appears on the document.

address.description: Postal address captured from document. Each component is its own field for individual copy-paste.

state.description: 2-letter state code when visible on fax.

phone.description: Phone/fax number as shown. raw_text preserves formatting; digits is 10 digits only for dialing.

patient.description: Patient as identified on the fax. Does NOT include eCW account number or any downstream matcher output — the matcher runs after extraction and consumes this block + the identifiers to find the eCW patient record.

dob.description: ISO date YYYY-MM-DD. If OCR ambiguous or date not printed, omit this field; dob_raw is still populated.

dob_raw.description: Verbatim DOB string as printed, e.g. '04/17/1958' or '17-APR-1958'.

sex.description: Sex as printed on the document. Typical values: M, F, Male, Female, Unknown. Preserve verbatim.

identifiers.description: Patient identifiers printed on the fax (lab MRN, hospital MRN, insurance member ID, Medicare number, etc.). Captured as printed — no normalization, no namespacing. Matcher uses these downstream to find eCW record.

label.description: Label as printed: 'Lab MRN', 'Account #', 'Member ID', 'Medicare', 'SSN', 'Patient ID', etc.

provider.description: Provider as identified on the fax (ordering physician, interpreting radiologist, signing hospitalist, etc.). Only populated from text actually on the page.

role.description: Role as printed or implied by section label: 'Ordering', 'Attending', 'Referring', 'Signed by', 'PCP', 'Radiologist', etc.

credentials.description: Credentials as printed: ['MD'], ['DO', 'FACP'], ['NP'], ['PA-C']. One entry per credential.

npi.description: NPI ONLY if printed on the fax. Do not look up.

sender.description: Organization that sent the fax (lab, imaging center, hospital, pharmacy, law firm, payer, etc.) as identified on the document.

clia_number.description: CLIA ONLY if printed on the fax.

lab_director.description: Laboratory director name string, if printed.

fax_envelope.description: Transmission metadata about the fax itself from the fax service (Updox, SRFax, etc.) — not content from the document.

csid_banner.description: Top banner CSID string printed by the sending fax device, e.g. 'QUEST DIAGNOSTICS'.
